ops.title: --repo dirExists guard is a wrong-repo-write defense, not defensive coding
ops.body:
The global `--repo`/`-R` flag (commit be94d649) resolves via `repoDir(cmd)` in internal/cli/bootstrap.go, which rejects a set-but-nonexistent path with a UsageError **on purpose**: go-git's `PlainOpenWithOptions` with `DetectDotGit` (v5.19.1, repository.go:341-386) walks UP from a nonexistent start path with no existence check, so a typo'd `--repo` under any repo would silently open the ancestor repo and write to the wrong store — the exact cross-repo failure the flag exists to fix. Do not strip this guard as "defensive coding"; the litmus subtest in TestRepoFlag (nonexistent path under the repo must fail with the `--repo … not a directory` usage error) pins it.

Related settled decisions: `--repo` is elided from didyoumean's `acceptedFlagsLine` self-heal output alongside help/version (global infrastructure, not per-command vocabulary — keeps the line deterministic across parse states); `--repo ""` collapses to cwd (pflag value-emptiness idiom, an empty path is meaningless); `cc-notes mcp` keeps its own `--dir` with `--repo` as fallback below it; attachment/MOUNTPOINT file-path args stay resolved against the invocation cwd.
